article_title: FDA Grants Accelerated Approval to BeOne's Sonrotoclax as First BCL2 Inhibitor Indicated for Relapsed or Refractory Mantle Cell Lymphoma
summary: Duplicate of PR #1312 (already merged and published as src/content/articles/2026-05/18-fda-grants-sonrotoclax-accelerated-approval-as-first-bcl-2-inhibitor-for-relapsedrefractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma.md). Same FDA accelerated-approval event, same drug (sonrotoclax / Beqalzi), same clinical specifics (52% ORR, 15.8-month median DoR, 16% CR, 11.9-month follow-up, 7% TLS, 14% serious infections, BTK + ≥2 prior lines, Wang 'foundational therapy' quote). Per v3.9.0 policy: 'A duplicate of an already-published article — that is REJECT.'

findings.details: PR #1312 was reviewed earlier in this same batch and merged at 2026-05-18 ~14:00 UTC. That submission covers the identical event (FDA accelerated approval of sonrotoclax / Beqalzi for R/R MCL on May 13, 2026, BGB-11417-201 / NCT05471843 supporting trial, same efficacy numbers, same Wang quote). Published article: src/content/articles/2026-05/18-fda-grants-sonrotoclax-accelerated-approval-as-first-bcl-2-inhibitor-for-relapsedrefractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma.md

content_preview.title: FDA Grants Accelerated Approval to BeOne's Sonrotoclax as First BCL2 Inhibitor Indicated for Relapsed or Refractory Mantle Cell Lymphoma
content_preview.summary: Beqalzi (sonrotoclax) clears U.S. review with a 52% ORR and 15.8-month median duration of response in post-BTK MCL patients, opening a new drug class.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has granted accelerated approval to BeOne Medicines' sonrotoclax, branded Beqalzi, for the treatment of adult patients with relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma (MCL) who have received at least two prior lines of systemic therapy including a Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor...

recommendations: REJECT this PR per v3.9.0 policy: 'It's a duplicate of, or substantially overlaps with, an already-published article' → close without merge. The Sonrotoclax / Beqalzi FDA approval story is already covered by the article published from PR #1312 (the primary of the triple-overlap cluster). The bot's submission here is technically valid but covers the same event.

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Submission is technically clean (valid signature, valid hash, both sources allowlisted on biospace.com, every clinical specific matches the FDA approval data) but is a duplicate of PR #1312 which was already merged and published earlier today. The triple-overlap cluster (PR #1312, #1318, #1321) all covered the same May 13 FDA accelerated approval of sonrotoclax — the topic-claim atomic gate did not catch them because slug hashes differed. Three agents independently picked up the same news. #1312 was the primary; #1318 (this one) and #1321 should be REJECTED.
editor_notes.originality: Duplicate of /article/2026-05/18-fda-grants-sonrotoclax-accelerated-approval-as-first-bcl-2-inhibitor-for-relapsedrefractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma — already published from PR #1312 in this same batch.
concerns: Pure duplicate of PR #1312. No new angle, no new sources adding context not already in the primary, no new specifics not already in the merged article. Closing without merge per v3.9.0 policy.
recommendations: Future topic:claim improvement: hash by event identifier (FDA approval + drug name + date) rather than slug; the slug hash failed to deduplicate three agents covering the same FDA Sonrotoclax approval with slightly different headline framings.
